claim: Hand A EVA 'words' encode Latin syllables, not Latin words. Under this hypothesis, per-folio Hand-A token counts should correlate with the length of the corresponding plant's typical pharmaceutical entry — measured here via Sherwood/Cohen medicinal_use + notes field length as a proxy for canonical-source (Circa Instans) entry length.
evidence_for: H-BV-HAND-A-INTERNAL-01 confirmed inflectional morphology and topic structure — consistent with either word or syllable-level encoding
Pagel expansion refuted (H-BV-PAGEL-EXPANSION-01) — Latin word-to-word mapping does not scale; a syllable-level encoding would explain why word-level glossaries produce low-signal output
Hand A token counts per folio (~50-400) are broadly in the range expected for syllabic encoding of short-to-medium pharmaceutical entries
evidence_against: PROXY LIMITATION: Sherwood notes-length is a weak proxy for actual canonical-source entry length. The test conclusion is bounded by proxy quality.
If Hand A encodes something other than pharmaceutical descriptions, correlation with pharma-entry length fails regardless of encoding granularity
Latin-syllable encoding was not something Pagel or Brady proposed; the hypothesis is Brain-V-generated and may not match the manuscript's actual mechanism
test: LOCKED: (1) For each Hand-A plant folio with a Sherwood/Cohen plant-ID (non-conflict), compute x = Hand-A token count (>=20 threshold). (2) Compute y = length in characters of (medicinal_use + notes) fields, as proxy for canonical-source entry length. (3) Compute Pearson r between x and y across all qualifying Hand-A plant folios. (4) Also compute Spearman rho as non-parametric check.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if Pearson r > 0.5 AND Spearman rho > 0.4 (syllable-level mapping supported). MARGINAL if 0.3 < r <= 0.5. REFUTED if r <= 0.3 (test H-BV-HOMOPHONIC-01 next).

tests_run.details: n=88 Hand-A plant folios. Pearson r=+0.0589 (p=0.586), Spearman rho=+0.0454 (p=0.674). Essentially zero correlation. REFUTED under locked rule r<=0.3. CAVEAT: proxy (Sherwood notes length) is weak — not actual Circa Instans. OBSERVATION: Hand-A folio token counts are tight (mean 79.5, sd 19.3, range 45-147) suggesting fixed page-length constraint OR content not plant-specific. Next test: homophonic substitution.

reasoning: After Pagel expansion refuted word-level Latin mapping, the natural successor is syllable-level. The proxy (Sherwood notes length) is weak but gives SOMETHING to test. A strong correlation under a weak proxy would be all the more compelling; a null correlation under a weak proxy is evidence but not definitive.
